function_reachability: Is it impossible for function 'malloc' to reach function 'free'?

dominators: Does the statement line 23:  <statement> free(X[i][j]); </statement> dominate line 19:  <statement> for(k =0; k < n; k++) </statement>?
Does the statement line 11:  <statement> X[i] = (int **) malloc(n * sizeof(int *)); </statement> dominate line 13:  <statement> X[i][j] = (int *) malloc(n *sizeof(int)); </statement>?
data_dependency: Does the value of variable X at line 9: <statement> 	X = (int ***) malloc(n * sizeof(int **)); </statement> depend on the value of variable n at line 7: <statement> 	int n = 10; </statement>?
Does variable i at line 17: <statement> 	for (i=0; i<n; i++) </statement> determine the value stored in variable j at line 18: <statement> 		for (j=0; j<n; j++) </statement>?
liveness: Does variable 'X' remain in use at the end of function 'main'?

dead_code: Can the statement 'X[i][j][k] = i;' be considered dead code?
